metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1833
metadata.comment:
Rationale: 6/11/2017 Pim

Prior information:
- NRPs are known binders of semaphorins. NRP-1 is necessary for Sema3A-mediated repulsive guidance events (Raper, 2000; Pasterkamp and Kolodkin, 2003; Fujisawa, 2004)

(not shown): sNRP-1 blocked the promoting effect of Sema3A on branching formation
Fig.3: Exposure to Sema3A increases PSD-95 immunoreactive clusters in cultured cortical neurons, and sNRP-1 (dom.neg.) blocked the promoting effect of Sema3A on PSD-95 cluster formation (overexpression, cortical neurons)
Fig.4: Colocalization of clusters of PSD-95 and NRP-1 on spine-like protrusions on dendrites. (overexpression, cortical neurons)
Fig.5: Sema3A increases PSD-95- and synapsin I-immunoreactive clusters in cultured cortical neurons (overexpression, cortical neurons). Experimental description: Mouse cortical cultures are used for the staining.

Literal From M&M:
"Primary cortical culture. Cortical neurons derived from embryonic day 16.5 (E16.5) brains of ICR mice were plated on four-well dishes (Nalgen, Rochester, NY) coated with poly-L-lysine (Sigma) at a density of 4-7x10000 cells per well and grown in Neurobasal medium supplemented with B27 and L-glutamine."

6/11/2017 Pim
- Overexpressed Sema3A is of unknown species. Author was contacted (goshima@med.yokohama-cu.ac.jp, dd 6/11/2017) for species information. Authors answered:

"I am Yukio Sasaki, one of authors entitled "Regulation of dendritic branching and spine maturation by semaphorin3A-Fyn signaling". We used "chick" Sema3A in this paper.
Sincerely,
Yukio Sasaki"
